facts.text: In 1954, the Hoffman Clock Museum opened inside Newark Public Library, showcasing a unique collection of antique clocks.
Newark is the birthplace of the Jackson & Perkins Company, founded in 1872 by Albert Jackson and his son‑in‑law Charles H., a pioneer in rose cultivation.
The village’s historic Erie Canal locks, including Lock 28B, still welcome boaters and are part of the Port of Newark Interpretive Center.
The Jackson–Perkins House at 310 High Street, once the company’s headquarters, is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
The Newark‑Arcadia Historical Society Museum, located a short walk from the canal, preserves local history and hosts rotating exhibitions.
Newark was incorporated as a village in 1853, making it one of the oldest communities along the Erie Canal.
